Edward King
Writing on Teaching, Engineering & Learning
Writing About

Essays on teaching, engineering, and the places where they meet. I spent four years writing C++ for production firmware, then became a computer science teacher, and I am still working out what each role taught me about the other.

Writing

What MMORPGs Taught Me About Learning

April 8, 2025
games learning

When I was learning to play a new MMO several years ago, I noticed something that has stuck with me as a teacher: I was failing constantly, and I was enjoying it. I would attempt content that was slightly beyond my current skill — a dun...

The Hidden Cost of Clever Code

January 20, 2025
engineering

There is a kind of code that experienced programmers learn to dread. It is not bad code — it is not sloppy or broken or careless. It is, in fact, technically impressive. It is dense with knowledge: obscure language features, elegant abst...

On Teaching Students to Debug

September 15, 2024
teaching engineering

The first thing I noticed when I started teaching computer science was that students do not know how to be wrong. They type a line of code, run it, see an error, and immediately start changing things — deleting, rewriting, guessing — wit...
